stem: A urban planning study measures the commute time (minutes) for a sample of 25 city workers. The sample mean is 138.7 min with a sample standard deviation of 24.8 min. However, 3 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 22 observations have a mean of 133.1 min and standard deviation of 15.6 min. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 138.7, 95% CI = [128.5, 148.9]. Without outliers: mean = 133.1, 95% CI = [126.4, 139.8]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [126.4, 148.9]. Point estimate: 135.9 min.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [126.4, 148.9].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
